meta.title: End-of-Day Reflection Tree
meta.description: A fully deterministic decision tree walking employees through three psychological axes: Locus of Control, Contribution vs Entitlement, and Radius of Concern.
meta.branching_rule: 4 options per question collapse into 2 psychological groups → 2 branches. Some nodes are pass-throughs that store state without branching.

A1-Q1.label: Entry — Attribution Anchor
A1-Q1.text: When things didn't go your way today, which of these felt most true in the moment?

options.text: I probably could have done something differently
The situation just wasn't set up for success
I kept going — I knew effort would get me through
I felt like no matter what I did, the outcome was already decided

A1-Q1.branch_logic: A+C → INTERNAL → A1-Q2i | B+D → EXTERNAL → A1-Q2e

A1-Q2i.label: Internal Branch — Success Attribution
A1-Q2i.text: When you succeeded at something today — even something small — what do you think made it happen?

options.text: I prepared well or put in real effort before it
It played to something I'm naturally good at
Honestly, the conditions were just favorable today
I'm not sure — it came together but I don't fully know why

A1-Q2i.branch_logic: A → A1-Q3i-effort | B → A1-Q3i-ability | C+D → A1-Q3e
A1-Q2i.note: C+D: self-identifies as internal but attributes success externally — fixed mindset signal

A1-Q3i-effort.label: Effort Branch — Expectancy Shift
A1-Q3i-effort.text: If tomorrow's work is harder than today's, what's your honest expectation?

options.text: I'll push harder — effort is what got me here
I'll probably do about the same — I know my pace
I'm not sure — it depends on too many things outside me
Harder tasks usually mean worse outcomes for me

A1-Q3i-ability.label: Ability Branch — Feedback Orientation
A1-Q3i-ability.text: When you got feedback on your work recently, what was your first instinct?

options.text: I wanted to understand it so I could improve
I appreciated it but didn't feel I needed to change much
It felt unnecessary — I know my own strengths
It was hard to hear, even if it was fair

A1-Q2e.label: External Branch — Attribution Subtype
A1-Q2e.text: When something went wrong today, how did you explain it to yourself?

options.text: The task itself was set up in a way that made success unlikely
Timing and circumstances just weren't on my side
Other people didn't do what I needed them to do
I'm not sure I'm the right fit for this kind of work

A1-Q2e.branch_logic: A+B → SITUATIONAL → A1-Q3e-situational | C+D → PERSONAL → A1-Q3e-personal

A1-Q3e.label: Drift Catch — Agency Probe
A1-Q3e.text: If the same situation came up again tomorrow, what would you expect?

options.text: Same result — the conditions haven't changed
It might go differently — these things are unpredictable
I'd approach it differently and probably get a better outcome
I'd try to avoid being in that situation again

A1-Q3e.branch_logic: A → VICTIM-STABLE | B → VICTIM-UNCERTAIN | C → TRANSITIONAL | D → VICTIM-AVOIDANT

A1-Q3e-situational.label: Situational External — Expectancy Shift
A1-Q3e-situational.text: If the same situation came up again tomorrow, what would you expect?

options.text: Same result — the conditions haven't changed
It might go differently — these things are unpredictable
I'd approach it differently and probably get a better outcome
I'd try to avoid being in that situation again

A1-Q3e-situational.branch_logic: A → VICTIM-STABLE | B → VICTIM-UNCERTAIN | C → TRANSITIONAL | D → VICTIM-AVOIDANT

A1-Q3e-personal.label: Personal External — Agency Awareness
A1-Q3e-personal.text: How much do you think today's outcome could change if you approached it differently next time?

options.text: Quite a bit — I see specific things I could do differently
Maybe a little, but the bigger factors are still out of my hands
Not much — some things just aren't a match for me
I haven't really thought about it that way

A1-Q3e-personal.branch_logic: A → TRANSITIONAL | B → VICTIM-PARTIAL | C → VICTIM-STABLE | D → VICTIM-UNCERTAIN

A1-EXIT.text: Axis 1 complete. Exit state selects Axis 2 entry variant.

A2-Q1-victor-effort.text: You pushed hard today. When you did — who else benefited from that effort besides yourself?

options.text: I stepped in without being asked — it felt natural
I helped, but only after I finished what I was already doing
I noticed but stayed focused on my own work — it wasn't my responsibility
I didn't really notice anyone needing anything today

A2-Q1-victor-growth.text: You stayed open to feedback today. Did that openness create space for anyone else around you?

options.text: Yes — I encouraged others to share their views too
Somewhat — I was focused on my own growth but stayed collaborative
Not really — I was working on myself today
I didn't think about it in relation to others

A2-Q1-fixed.text: You owned today's outcomes. Was there a moment where someone else's outcome was also in your hands?

options.text: Yes — and I took that seriously
Maybe — I helped where I could but kept my main focus
I don't think so — everyone manages their own outcomes
I wasn't thinking about others' outcomes today

A2-Q1-uncertain.text: Even on uncertain days, people still give. What did you put in today that wasn't required of you?

options.text: I helped someone even though I had my own pressure to deal with
I did a bit extra but stayed close to what was needed
I focused on getting through my own tasks — that felt like enough
Honestly nothing extra — today was about survival

A2-Q1-transitional.text: You showed more agency than you might have given yourself credit for. Did any of that agency go toward someone else?

options.text: Yes — I used what I had to make things easier for someone around me
A little — I stayed collaborative even when things were hard
Not really — I was focused on managing my own situation
I didn't have bandwidth to think about others today

A2-Q1-victim-stable.text: Today felt largely outside your control. Was there anything — however small — that you chose to give anyway?

options.text: Yes — I still showed up for someone even when things were hard
I tried to — it was small but I made the effort
Not really — I was just trying to get through the day
No — I had nothing left to give today

A2-Q1-victim-uncertain.text: On days that feel unpredictable, it's easy to go inward. Did anyone else cross your mind today?

options.text: Yes — I checked in on someone or offered help
Someone came to mind but I wasn't sure how to help
Not really — I was caught in my own head today
No — today was hard enough to navigate alone

A2-Q1-victim-partial.text: Some things were in your control today, some weren't. In the parts that were — did anyone else factor into your decisions?

options.text: Yes — I thought about how my choices would affect others
A little — I tried to be considerate where I could
Not consciously — I was focused on solving my own piece
No — I was managing what I could and that was enough

A2-Q1-victim-avoidant.text: Avoiding difficult situations is a real response. Was there a moment today where you stayed present for someone else even when it was uncomfortable?

options.text: Yes — I pushed through discomfort to support someone
I tried — it wasn't easy but I made the effort
Not really — I needed to protect my own space today
No — I kept to myself as much as possible

A2-Q2c.label: Contribution Branch — Motivation Probe
A2-Q2c.text: When you helped or contributed today, what was honestly driving it?

options.text: I genuinely wanted the other person or team to succeed
It felt like the right thing to do in the moment
I knew it would reflect well on me or strengthen a relationship
It was expected of me — not helping would have looked bad

A2-Q2c.branch_logic: A+B → A2-Q3c-deep | C → A2-Q3c-strategic | D → A2-Q3e

A2-Q3c-deep.label: Altruistic Branch — Invisible Effort
A2-Q3c-deep.text: Did you do anything today that no one will notice or credit you for?

options.text: Yes — and that's fine, it still mattered
Yes, but I'll admit I noticed that it went unrecognized
No, but not intentionally — it just didn't come up
Probably not — I tend to focus on what I'm accountable for

A2-Q3c-deep.branch_logic: A → CONTRIBUTOR-DEEP | B → CONTRIBUTOR-AWARE | C → CONTRIBUTOR-SITUATIONAL | D → CONTRIBUTOR-BOUNDED

A2-Q3c-strategic.label: Strategic Branch — Recognition Response
A2-Q3c-strategic.text: When your contribution today wasn't acknowledged, how did you feel?

options.text: Neutral — the outcome mattered more than the credit
Mildly disappointed but I moved on
It bothered me more than I'd like to admit
I made sure it was acknowledged before the day ended

A2-Q3c-strategic.branch_logic: A → CONTRIBUTOR-DEEP | B → CONTRIBUTOR-AWARE | C → ENTITLEMENT-AWARE | D → ENTITLEMENT-ACTIVE

A2-Q2e.label: Entitlement Branch — Subtype Identification
A2-Q2e.text: Thinking about today — which of these thoughts crossed your mind at any point?

options.text: I felt my effort wasn't being fairly recognized compared to others
I wondered if I was being given work that was beneath my level
I felt like I was carrying more than my fair share
None of these — I just wasn't thinking about others much today

A2-Q2e.branch_logic: A+B → A2-Q3e-recognition | C → A2-Q3e-effort | D → A2-Q3e-awareness

A2-Q3e-recognition.label: Recognition Entitlement — Behavioral Response
A2-Q3e-recognition.text: When a colleague received recognition today that you felt you deserved equally or more, what did you do?

options.text: Nothing — their recognition didn't affect mine
I felt it but kept it to myself and refocused
I brought up my own contributions in a later conversation
It genuinely affected my motivation for the rest of the day

A2-Q3e-recognition.branch_logic: A+B → ENTITLEMENT-AWARE | C → ENTITLEMENT-ACTIVE | D → ENTITLEMENT-DEEP

A2-Q3e-effort.label: Martyrdom Branch — Effort Response Under Inequity
A2-Q3e-effort.text: You felt you were carrying more than your share today. Did that change how much effort you put in?

options.text: No — I kept the same standard regardless
I pulled back slightly to match what others were doing
I pushed through but felt resentful about it
I raised it with someone rather than letting it build

A2-Q3e-effort.branch_logic: A → CONTRIBUTOR-AWARE | B → ENTITLEMENT-ACTIVE | C → ENTITLEMENT-MARTYR | D → CONTRIBUTOR-BOUNDED

A2-Q3e-awareness.label: Low Awareness Branch — Noticing Probe
A2-Q3e-awareness.text: Today, was there a moment where a colleague was struggling and you were aware of it?

options.text: Yes — and I checked in or offered something
Yes — but I assumed they'd handle it or ask if they needed help
I'm not sure — I was pretty heads-down today
Not that I noticed

A2-Q3e-awareness.branch_logic: A → CONTRIBUTOR-SITUATIONAL | B → ENTITLEMENT-AWARE | C+D → LOW-AWARENESS

A2-Q3e.label: Obligatory Contribution — Feeling Probe
A2-Q3e.text: When you contributed out of obligation today, how did you feel about the lack of acknowledgment?

options.text: Fine — I don't need recognition for doing what's expected
A little drained — I'd appreciate more acknowledgment
Frustrated — if it's expected, it should at least be appreciated
I'm not sure — I didn't think much about it either way

A2-Q3e.branch_logic: A → CONTRIBUTOR-BOUNDED | B → ENTITLEMENT-AWARE | C → ENTITLEMENT-ACTIVE | D → CONTRIBUTOR-SITUATIONAL

A2-EXIT.text: Axis 2 complete. Exit state selects Axis 3 entry variant.

A3-Q1-contributor-deep.text: You gave today without keeping score. When you were giving — whose face or situation were you holding in mind?

options.text: A specific colleague — I knew exactly who I was helping
My team generally — I was thinking about all of us
A customer or end user I may never meet
No one specific — it was more about doing the right thing

A3-Q1-contributor-aware.text: You contributed meaningfully today. Did your thinking extend beyond the people immediately around you?

options.text: Yes — I thought about someone specific outside my immediate team
Mostly within my team — that felt like the right scope
Yes — I thought about who the work ultimately serves
Not really — I was focused on what was in front of me

A3-Q1-contributor-situational.text: Is there someone further from you — a customer, an end user — who was affected by your work today without you directly seeing it?

options.text: Yes — I can trace my work to a specific person or group
Probably — but I don't often think about it that way
Maybe — it's hard to see the connection from where I sit
I honestly hadn't thought about it

A3-Q1-contributor-bounded.text: You contributed within your lane today. Was there anyone outside that lane who your work quietly touched?

options.text: Yes — I can see how my work connects to someone beyond my team
Possibly — there are people downstream I don't always think about
Within my immediate team, yes
I stay focused on what's assigned — connections beyond that are unclear

A3-Q1-entitlement-aware.text: Some frustration is natural. In the moments between that frustration — did anyone else's reality enter your thinking?

options.text: Yes — I noticed someone else having a harder time than me
I thought about how the team was feeling overall
I thought about who ultimately depends on us getting this right
Mostly not — I was working through my own experience

A3-Q1-entitlement-active.text: Today had moments of friction around recognition. Separate from that — who depended on your work today, even invisibly?

options.text: A colleague who needed what I produced to do their own work
The team — our outputs are interconnected
Customers or stakeholders I don't interact with directly
I'm not sure — I don't always see who depends on me downstream

A3-Q1-entitlement-martyr.text: You carried a lot today. Before we close — was there one person who had it harder than you, that you noticed?

options.text: Yes — and I actually thought about what they were dealing with
I noticed the team was stretched — not just me
I thought about who we're all ultimately doing this for
Honestly, I was too absorbed in my own load to notice

A3-Q1-entitlement-deep.text: Today was heavy. Even so — is there one person whose day was made even slightly better because you showed up?

options.text: Yes — I can name them
Probably someone on the team, though I'm not sure who
Someone downstream — my work matters even when it doesn't feel like it
I'm not sure anyone's day was different because of me today

A3-Q1-low-awareness.text: You were heads-down today — that's real work. If your work today had a ripple effect, who do you think felt it first?

options.text: A specific colleague who depends on my output
The team — we're all connected whether we see it or not
Customers or users I don't interact with directly
I genuinely don't know — the connection isn't visible from where I sit

A3-Q2.label: Radius Confirmation — Whose Reality Entered Your Mind
A3-Q2.text: During today, whose situation genuinely entered your mind — not just as a task, but as a person?

options.text: A specific colleague — I was aware of what they were dealing with
My team generally — I was thinking about how we were doing collectively
A customer or end user — I thought about how my work lands for them
Mostly just my own work and what I needed to get done

A3-Q2.branch_logic: A → A3-Q3a | B → A3-Q3b | C → A3-Q3c | D → A3-Q3d

A3-Q3a.label: Interpersonal Radius — Empathy vs Sympathy
A3-Q3a.text: When that person crossed your mind — what did you do with that awareness?

options.text: I reached out or did something concrete for them
I held it in mind but didn't act — I wasn't sure if I should
I noted it but stayed focused on my own work
I felt for them but assumed they'd be fine

A3-Q3a.branch_logic: A+B → A3-Q4-depth | C → RADIUS-INTERPERSONAL-PASSIVE | D → RADIUS-INTERPERSONAL-SYMPATHY
A3-Q3a.note: Batson 2011 — empathy involves modeling the other's reality and responding. D is sympathy (feeling for, not with).

A3-Q3b.label: Team Radius — Orientation Frame
A3-Q3b.text: When you thought about the team today, what was the frame?

options.text: I was thinking about what the team needed to succeed collectively
I was aware of the team's mood or energy and how it affected the work
I was thinking about my role within the team and how I was performing
I thought about the team mostly in relation to what I needed from them

A3-Q3b.branch_logic: A+B → A3-Q4-depth | C → RADIUS-TEAM-SELFFILTERED | D → RADIUS-SELF-INSTRUMENTAL

A3-Q3c.label: Mission Radius — Transcendence Trigger
A3-Q3c.text: When you thought about the customer or end user today, what triggered it?

options.text: I naturally think about them — it's part of how I approach my work
A specific moment made me stop and consider their experience
Someone else brought them up and it shifted my thinking
I thought about them in terms of metrics or outcomes, not as a person

A3-Q3c.branch_logic: A → RADIUS-MISSION-DEEP | B → A3-Q4-depth | C → RADIUS-MISSION-EMERGING | D → RADIUS-TEAM-SELFFILTERED
A3-Q3c.note: A = Maslow self-transcendence as default orientation. B = peak experience moment (Maslow).

A3-Q3d.label: Self Radius — Focus Cause
A3-Q3d.text: You were focused on your own work today. What was driving that focus?

options.text: I had a lot to deliver and the pressure was real
That's generally how I work — I focus on my own output
I wasn't aware of much beyond my immediate tasks
I prefer to stay in my lane — others can manage their own work

A3-Q3d.branch_logic: A → RADIUS-SELF-CIRCUMSTANTIAL | B+C → A3-Q4-reframe | D → RADIUS-SELF-DELIBERATE
A3-Q3d.note: A = overwhelm not selfishness. D = philosophical. B+C need prompted reframe.

A3-Q4-depth.label: Depth Probe — Impact Localization
A3-Q4-depth.text: If your work today disappeared — was never done — who would have felt that absence most?
A3-Q4-depth.note: Most powerful question in the axis — forces the employee to locate their work's meaning in another person's experience.

options.text: A specific person I can name
My team or a group of people
Customers or people I don't directly interact with
Honestly, I'm not sure — I haven't thought about it that way

A3-Q4-depth.branch_logic: A → RADIUS-INTERPERSONAL-DEEP | B → RADIUS-TEAM-DEEP | C → RADIUS-MISSION-DEEP | D → RADIUS-EMERGING

A3-Q4-reframe.label: Reframe Probe — Prompted Transcendence
A3-Q4-reframe.text: Think about one person — inside or outside the company — whose day was different because you showed up today. Who comes closest?

options.text: A colleague I directly worked with
Someone I helped even briefly
I'm not sure anyone's day was different because of me today
Someone who will feel the effect of my work later, not today

A3-Q4-reframe.branch_logic: A+B → RADIUS-EMERGING | C → RADIUS-SELF-DEEP | D → RADIUS-MISSION-EMERGING
A3-Q4-reframe.note: A+B: self-focused person found someone when prompted — celebrate in summary.

A3-EXIT.text: Axis 3 complete. All three axes done. Proceed to summary generation.

SUMMARY.text: Final node. Reflection summary generated from full accumulated state. See reflections.json for interpolated templates.
